name: NgRx (Angular State Management with RxJS)

auth.notes: Angular state management library; no auth concept

performance.notes: RxJS-based; overhead from action dispatch cycle is minimal for typical use

agent_readiness.notes: Redux-inspired Angular state management. import { createAction, createReducer, createSelector, createEffect, on, props } from '@ngrx/store'. const increment = createAction('[Counter] Increment'). const counterReducer = createReducer(0, on(increment, state => state + 1)). Store: store.dispatch(increment()). store.select(selectCount). createSelector for memoized selectors. @ngrx/effects for side effects: createEffect(() => actions$.pipe(ofType(load), mergeMap(() => service.getItems()))). @ngrx/entity for normalized collections. @ngrx/router-store for routing. @ngrx/component-store for local state. @ngrx/signals for Angular Signals integration (v17+). StoreModule.forRoot() / StoreModule.forFeature(). DevTools integration.

gotchas: Very verbose for simple state — many files per feature (actions, effects, reducers, selectors)
Steep learning curve: Redux, RxJS, and Angular all required simultaneously
Over-engineered for small/medium Angular apps — use component state or signals instead
Boilerplate was reduced in v15+ but still significant vs zustand/Pinia
@ngrx/signals is newer — NgRx approach is shifting with Angular Signals adoption
best_when: Large Angular enterprise apps needing predictable state management
Teams already experienced with Redux pattern
When time-travel debugging (NgRx DevTools) is required
Complex async flows via NgRx Effects with RxJS operators
avoid_when: Small/medium Angular apps — use services or @ngrx/component-store
New Angular projects — consider Angular Signals-first approach first
Non-Angular projects (use Redux Toolkit, Zustand, or Pinia)
summary: NgRx provides Redux-inspired state management for Angular apps. createAction, createReducer, createSelector, createEffect. @ngrx/effects for async side effects. @ngrx/entity for normalized collections. @ngrx/signals for Angular Signals. MIT by NgRx Team. Best for large Angular enterprise apps. Very verbose for simple state. Steep learning curve. New Angular projects may prefer Signals-first approach.
